mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS levels (below 0.25%) signal an extended accommodative monetary stance that lowers the equity discount rate and sustains risk asset valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as long as the funds rate stays anchored near the zero lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% as of 2015-03-01 vintage, near zero lower bound since 2008
regime_hint: any regime with near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60d SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or positive-return hit rate during ZIRP periods drops below the unconditional 65.5% base rate.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals an inflection toward labor-market deterioration consistent with early recession dynamics, and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as markets reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: When the 0.3pp trigger fires, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC conditional on the trigger is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS month-over-month growth and INDPRO month-over-month growth (both falling below their trailing 12-month average growth rates concurrently) jointly signals a broad-based real-economy slowdown that single-series momentum in either variable alone fails to capture, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS 141183K vintage vs 140923K revised, INDPRO 105.2161 vintage vs 101.8914 revised as of March 2015
regime_hint: any regime, strongest in mid-to-late expansion
failure_condition: The joint deceleration signal (both PAYEMS and INDPRO below trailing average growth) produces forward 60d returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or a single-series model using either PAYEMS or INDPRO alone matches or exceeds the joint model's Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS retail sales growth combined with a concurrently accommodative FEDFUNDS regime (below 0.5%) amplifies consumer-demand-driven earnings optimism, producing stronger positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns than retail sales strength would generate under a tightening monetary regime, because low policy rates remove the competing discount-rate headwind.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS 441363M vintage vs 433647M revised, FEDFUNDS 0.11% as of March 2015
failure_condition: During periods where RSAFS growth is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, forward 20d SPX returns underperform the unconditional average, or the interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS level shows no statistically significant incremental predictive power (p > 0.10) versus RSAFS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate revenue momentum, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as production data feeds directly into near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5d SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips persistently for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST housing starts fall below 900 thousand units (annualized), it signals weakening forward-looking residential investment and consumer confidence, a leading indicator of broader demand contraction that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as the housing-sensitive supply chain and construction employment channel transmits weakness to earnings expectations.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below the 900k threshold, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, or the conditional Rank IC is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL inflation combined with a FEDFUNDS rate held near zero implies falling real interest rates, which incentivizes reallocation from cash and bonds into equities as a reflation hedge, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days beyond what either inflation or nominal rates alone would predict.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL 235.186 vintage vs 235.342 revised, FEDFUNDS 0.11% as of early 2015
regime_hint: low real interest rate reflation regime
failure_condition: The joint falling-real-rate signal (rising CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS below 0.5%) fails to outperform a single-factor CPIAUCSL-only model in out-of-sample Rank IC, or forward 20d returns under the joint condition are not significantly different from the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle regime where UNRATE has already fallen near structural full-employment levels (below 5.5%), any subsequent deceleration in PAYEMS monthly job gains signals labor-market exhaustion rather than early-cycle noise, and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days because markets interpret slowing hiring near full employment as a precursor to margin compression.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5.5% and PAYEMS 3-month average job gains decelerate versus prior 3-month average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE 5.4% revised as of March 2015, approaching pre-crisis full-employment range
failure_condition: Within periods satisfying the UNRATE-below-5.5% and PAYEMS-deceleration trigger, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the conditional Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across any 3-year rolling walk-forward window.
